name: Boston
description: Boston, Massachusetts — history, culture, and sports

questions.q: Boston is the capital of which U.S. state?

source.notes: Has been the capital since the Massachusetts Bay Colony.

questions.q: In what year did the Boston Tea Party protest take place?

source.notes: December 16, 1773; Sons of Liberty dumped 342 chests of tea into Boston Harbor.

questions.q: Who made a famous midnight ride through the Massachusetts countryside on April 18, 1775 to warn of approaching British troops?

source.notes: Immortalized in Longfellow's 1860 poem 'Paul Revere's Ride'.

questions.q: Boston is located in which cultural/geographical region of the United States?

source.notes: Largest city in New England.

questions.q: Which river separates Boston from the neighboring city of Cambridge?

source.notes: About 80 miles long; flows into Boston Harbor.

questions.q: What do you call a resident of Boston?

source.notes: Alternate: 'Townie' for someone from a specific neighborhood.

questions.q: Boston's nickname 'Beantown' refers to which traditional local dish?

source.notes: Slow-baked navy beans sweetened with molasses.

questions.q: Boston's Major League Baseball team is known by what name?

source.notes: Play home games at Fenway Park — the oldest major league stadium in use.

questions.q: Boston's NBA basketball team is the what?

source.notes: Most NBA championships of any franchise (18 as of 2024).

questions.q: The NFL team based in the Boston area is called what?

source.notes: Play in Foxborough, Massachusetts; six Super Bowl wins.

questions.q: Boston's NHL team goes by which name?

source.notes: Founded 1924; six Stanley Cup championships.

questions.q: The city of Boston takes its name from a town in which English county?

source.notes: Many Puritan founders had connections to Boston in Lincolnshire.

questions.q: Boston Common, established in 1634, holds what distinction?
choices: Oldest public park in the United States
Largest urban park in North America

source.notes: About 50 acres in downtown Boston.

questions.q: What is the name of the oldest public school in the United States, founded in Boston in 1635?

source.notes: Alumni include Benjamin Franklin, Samuel Adams, and John Hancock.

questions.q: The Boston Tea Party was a protest against which British law of 1773?

source.notes: Gave the East India Company a monopoly on tea in the colonies.

questions.q: In what year did British troops fire on a crowd in what became known as the Boston Massacre?

source.notes: March 5, 1770; killed five colonists. Used as revolutionary propaganda.

questions.q: Boston's sister city of Cambridge, across the Charles River, is home to which two prestigious universities?

source.notes: Harvard is in Cambridge; MIT is in Cambridge; both founded before 1870.

questions.q: The Boston Marathon, the world's oldest annual marathon, is held on which Monday each year?

source.notes: Third Monday in April; first held 1897.

questions.q: The city of Boston was originally founded in 1630 on what peninsula?

source.notes: Original name given by Algonquin peoples.

questions.q: What is Boston's highest natural point?

source.notes: Rises about 330 feet in West Roxbury.

questions.q: What notable 1919 Boston disaster saw a huge wave of molasses flood parts of the North End?

source.notes: Storage tank burst January 15, 1919; killed 21 people.

questions.q: Approximately what fraction of Boston's modern land area was created through historical landfill projects?

source.notes: Back Bay, South End, and much of downtown sit on reclaimed land.
